reasoning: D wins on the strength of its concepts: SUTURE (a hardening 3D lattice of frozen straight trajectories that becomes a geometry/cat's-cradle sport) is the single most original mechanic in the packet, and WICK genuinely reframes falling as a portable wandering "down" you chase rather than just deleting gravity, which most slots only manage for one of their five. Its five sit on cleanly separated axes and KEEL even pre-solves its own spectator problem with a belief-vs-reality HUD, showing rare generativity. A is a close second: SUMP (bluffing about hidden mass, poker played with conservation of momentum) and SHROUD (a socially-constructed playing surface) are bold, vivid standouts, and its craft matches D's. C ranks third on the back of PARALLAX (loss of a shared reference frame as the core obstacle) and the elegant "winning makes you fragile" twist in BALLAST, but several of its games (TETHER, BALLAST, ring-based QUORUM) overlap conceptually with B's, denting diversity. B is last: ECHO is essentially billiards-dodgeball, SECOND SKIN is clever but its "falling" entry is muddy and abstract, making it the safest and least risk-taking set.
